rows.one_liner: Consumer symptom education app that explains common symptoms and suggests when to consult a doctor.
rows.label_rationale: General symptom education without diagnosis or treatment recommendation is likely non-device. Class A may apply if positioned as low-risk health information software.

rows.one_liner: Remote patient monitoring dashboard that displays vitals from connected home devices.
rows.label_rationale: Real-time vitals display with threshold-based alerts for clinician review is active patient monitoring, so Class B is the safer label. Class A would only fit passive fitness-style display without clinical alerts.

rows.one_liner: AI system that screens retinal fundus images for diabetic retinopathy risk.
rows.label_rationale: AI diabetic retinopathy screening influences diagnosis/referral for a serious condition. Class B may apply only if strictly preliminary triage with mandatory specialist review.

rows.one_liner: AI model that detects pneumonia patterns from chest X-rays.
rows.label_rationale: AI chest X-ray pneumonia detection can drive clinical diagnosis and treatment for serious disease. Class D may apply if used for emergency or autonomous diagnosis.

rows.one_liner: Hospital billing, inventory, and administrative workflow management platform.
rows.label_rationale: Hospital billing, inventory, and administrative workflow software is not intended for medical diagnosis, treatment, or monitoring. It should remain outside CDSCO device classification.

rows.one_liner: Medication adherence app that reminds patients to take prescribed medicines and alerts caregivers.
rows.label_rationale: Reminder-only medication adherence app without active dose recommendation is wellness/non-device. Class A may apply if positioned as low-risk health information software with any borderline clinical positioning.

rows.one_liner: AI system that analyzes ECG signals and flags possible arrhythmias.
rows.label_rationale: AI ECG arrhythmia detection supports diagnosis of potentially serious cardiovascular conditions. Class B may apply if it only flags recordings for clinician review without diagnostic claims.

rows.one_liner: Closed-loop software that recommends or controls insulin delivery based on CGM readings.
rows.label_rationale: Standalone insulin dose recommendation is high-risk treatment decision software and should be treated at least as Class C with Class D-level safety controls. Class D may apply if integrated with or controlling a high-risk insulin pump/autonomous closed loop.

rows.one_liner: Digital physiotherapy app that guides prescribed exercises and tracks adherence.
rows.label_rationale: Digital physiotherapy guidance supports rehabilitation management. Class A may apply where it only provides exercise education and adherence tracking.

rows.one_liner: Hospital AI that predicts sepsis risk using vitals, labs, and EHR data.
rows.label_rationale: Sepsis prediction influences urgent clinical management for a critical condition. Class D may apply if alerts directly trigger emergency intervention without clinician confirmation.

rows.one_liner: Consumer nutrition and fitness coaching app with calorie tracking and activity plans.
rows.label_rationale: Consumer nutrition and fitness coaching is likely wellness-only. Class A may apply if explicit health-condition monitoring claims are introduced.

rows.one_liner: Portable spirometry app that captures lung function readings for remote review.
rows.label_rationale: Remote spirometry supports clinical monitoring of respiratory disease. Class C may apply if diagnostic or treatment escalation claims are made.

rows.one_liner: Mobile imaging AI that flags suspicious oral lesions for referral.
rows.label_rationale: AI oral cancer screening supports detection/referral for a serious condition. Class B may apply only for very limited triage with clear clinician confirmation.

rows.one_liner: ABDM consent manager that enables consent-based health record exchange.
rows.label_rationale: ABDM consent manager or health record exchange software is health IT infrastructure, not a medical device. No CDSCO device class should be assigned.

rows.one_liner: Smartphone app that tracks wound healing progress and sends updates to clinicians.
rows.label_rationale: Wound monitoring software supports clinical monitoring and follow-up. Class C may apply if it diagnoses infection or recommends treatment changes.

rows.one_liner: Mobile app that logs glucose readings and gives lifestyle nudges for diabetics.
rows.label_rationale: Glucose trend visualization for diabetes self-management is low-risk chronic disease monitoring software. It may be non-device only if claims are restricted to wellness logging without diabetes management language.

rows.one_liner: AI model that reads ECG images and flags possible myocardial infarction.
rows.label_rationale: ECG myocardial infarction triage drives clinical management for a serious or critical condition. Class D may apply if used for emergency diagnosis without adequate clinician review.

rows.one_liner: Smartphone-based skin lesion classifier for suspicious mole detection.
rows.label_rationale: Skin cancer risk flagging influences diagnosis and referral for a serious condition. This fits Class C SaMD.

rows.one_liner: XR-based surgical planning tool for orthopedic implant placement.
rows.label_rationale: Surgical planning software influences clinical management but remains clinician-supervised. Class C may apply if it directly determines implant placement or surgical action.

rows.one_liner: Home fetal heartbeat listening device connected to a mobile app.
rows.label_rationale: Home fetal heartbeat monitoring can influence pregnancy care-seeking. Class C may apply if diagnostic fetal distress claims are made.

rows.one_liner: AI chatbot for stress journaling and mental wellness support.
rows.label_rationale: Non-clinical mental wellness journaling is likely outside medical device scope. If it claims mental health screening or clinical management, Class A may be the lower-bound class.

rows.one_liner: AI model that analyzes CT brain scans to detect possible stroke.
rows.label_rationale: Stroke CT triage relates to a critical condition and can drive urgent clinical management. Class C may apply if strictly prioritization-only with radiologist confirmation.

rows.one_liner: Tool that helps clinics create ABHA-linked digital health records.
rows.label_rationale: ABDM record creation and exchange is health IT infrastructure. It does not diagnose, treat, or monitor a disease.

rows.one_liner: AI tool that suggests likely cancer grading from pathology slide images.
rows.label_rationale: Cancer grading support influences diagnosis and clinical management for a serious disease. Class D may apply if output is used as primary diagnosis.

rows.one_liner: Computer vision app that tracks physiotherapy exercise adherence.
rows.label_rationale: Physiotherapy tracking supports rehabilitation management but is not generally life-critical. Class A may apply if only adherence or wellness feedback is claimed.

rows.one_liner: Predictive model that alerts ICU teams about patient deterioration risk.
rows.label_rationale: ICU deterioration prediction drives clinical management in a critical care environment. Class D may apply if the alert directly triggers emergency intervention.

rows.one_liner: AI assistant that helps hospitals prepare insurance pre-authorization documents.
rows.label_rationale: Insurance pre-authorization documentation is administrative and does not influence clinical diagnosis or treatment. It should not be classified as a medical device.

rows.one_liner: Software that calculates pediatric drug dosage based on age, weight, and diagnosis.
rows.label_rationale: Pediatric drug dose calculation affects medication safety and clinical treatment. Class B may apply only for transparent calculator-style support with full clinician verification.

rows.one_liner: Wearable ring that tracks sleep quality, HRV, and recovery.
rows.label_rationale: Consumer sleep, HRV, and recovery tracking is wellness-only if no diagnosis or treatment claims are made. Class A may apply if medical monitoring claims are introduced.

rows.one_liner: AI tool that screens chest X-rays for signs of pulmonary tuberculosis.
rows.label_rationale: TB chest X-ray screening supports detection of a serious infectious disease. Class B may apply if used only for workflow prioritization and not diagnostic output.

rows.one_liner: Dashboard that predicts dengue outbreak risk using weather and case data.
rows.label_rationale: Dengue hotspot forecasting is population-level public health planning, not patient-specific diagnosis, treatment, or monitoring. It should remain outside CDSCO medical device classification.

rows.one_liner: LLM assistant for nurses to summarize patient notes and suggest care checklist items.
rows.label_rationale: Nursing checklist generation can influence clinical workflow and patient management. Class A may apply if documentation-only with mandatory human review.

rows.one_liner: Mobile imaging tool that flags possible oral cancer lesions.
rows.label_rationale: Oral cancer lesion flagging supports screening and referral for a serious condition. This fits Class C SaMD.

rows.one_liner: AI tool that predicts OPD crowding and suggests appointment slots.
rows.label_rationale: OPD queue optimization is hospital operations software and does not diagnose, treat, or monitor patients. It should remain outside CDSCO medical device scope.

rows.one_liner: Wearable patch that continuously estimates blood pressure.
rows.label_rationale: Continuous blood pressure estimation supports monitoring and management of hypertension. Class B may apply if only trend display is claimed without clinical decision support.

rows.one_liner: AI tool that predicts high-risk pregnancy from ANC records.
rows.label_rationale: High-risk pregnancy prediction influences care prioritization for maternal health. Class B may apply if used only for non-diagnostic referral support.

rows.one_liner: Consumer app that explains lab reports in simple language.
rows.label_rationale: Lab report explanation that interprets clinical values into health status crosses from pure education into low-risk interpretive medical software. It may be non-device only if it strictly avoids interpretation and provides generic educational content.

rows.one_liner: App that recommends insulin dose changes based on CGM and meal logs.
rows.label_rationale: Insulin dose recommendation directly drives therapy for a potentially life-threatening condition. This should be treated as Class D SaMD.

rows.one_liner: WhatsApp bot that books appointments and collects patient intake details.
rows.label_rationale: Appointment booking and intake automation is administrative. It is not intended for medical diagnosis, treatment, or monitoring.

rows.one_liner: Connected nebulizer that tracks medication sessions and adherence.
rows.label_rationale: A smart nebulizer app that tracks treatment adherence is Class B patient/treatment support. Class C applies if the software adjusts nebulization sessions, controls therapy, or changes treatment instructions.

rows.one_liner: Software that generates orthodontic aligner plans from dental scans.
rows.label_rationale: Orthodontic aligner treatment planning influences therapeutic intervention. Class B may apply only if it is simulation-only and not used to finalize treatment plans.

rows.one_liner: Wearable fall detection device for elderly users.
rows.label_rationale: Fall detection and fall-prevention alarms are low-risk safety monitoring when they do not make neurological or diagnostic claims. Class B may apply only if positioned as clinical patient monitoring for a disease condition.

rows.one_liner: Clinical tool that recommends antibiotic choices based on symptoms and local resistance data.
rows.label_rationale: Antibiotic recommendation affects prescribing and treatment for potentially serious infections. Class D may apply where incorrect output could cause critical harm or antimicrobial failure.

rows.one_liner: Platform that matches patients to clinical trials using diagnosis and location.
rows.label_rationale: Clinical trial matching is informational if it does not recommend treatment. Class A may apply if eligibility pre-screening is positioned as medical decision support.

rows.one_liner: Portable spirometer with app-based COPD monitoring.
rows.label_rationale: Remote spirometry measures and monitors lung function. Class C may apply if diagnostic or treatment escalation claims are made.

synth.raw: {"meta":{"company_name":"Unknown","product_name":"Portable spirometer with app-based COPD monitoring","scoped_feature":null,"product_type":"hardware_software","generated_at":"2025-01-15T00:00:00Z","conflict_resolved":null},"classification":{"medical_device_status":"is_medical_device","device_type":"Portable spirometer (hardware) + companion app for COPD monitoring","imdrf_category":"II","cdsco_class":"B","class_qualifier":"scoped","ai_ml_flag":false,"acp_required":false,"export_only":false,"novel_or_predicate":"has_predicate"},"readiness":{"score":1,"band":"red","dimensions":{"regulatory_clarity":0,"quality_system":0,"technical_docs":1,"clinical_evidence":0,"submission_maturity":0},"note":"No detected signals around QMS, prior CDSCO engagement, or clinical validation. Some technical maturity assumed from a working prototype, but submission posture is effectively zero."},"risk":{"level":"high","rationale":"Spirometers are notified medical devices under CDSCO MDR 2017; a connected diagnostic device for a serious respiratory condition (COPD) sits clearly inside the regulated perimeter."},"timeline":{"estimate_months_low":12,"estimate_months_high":18,"display":"12–18 months","anchor":"Anchored to a typical Class B hardware+software pathway: QMS build-out, IEC 62304 documentation, bench testing, MD-5 application via state authority, and limited clinical performance data."},"regulations":{"cdsco_mdr":{"verdict":"required","rationale":"Spirometers are notified medical devices; the companion app likely inherits the hardware class as SiMD. Class B pathway is the most likely anchor based on published CDSCO guidance.","forms":["MD-5","MD-12"],"pathway_note":"Class B → state authority pathway (MD-5). Test license MD-12 may be needed for clinical investigation if no Indian predicate filing is leveraged."},"cdsco_pharmacy":{"verdict":"not_applicable","rationale":"No drug sale, dispensing, or distribution involved."},"dpdp":{"verdict":"required","rationale":"App collects patient respiratory and identity data; DPDP applies to any processing of Indian users' personal data, and health data is sensitive by default."},"icmr":{"verdict":"conditional","rationale":"No AI/ML claim detected. ICMR AI guidelines are not directly triggered, but EC oversight will apply if any prospective clinical performance study is run."},"abdm":{"verdict":"optional","rationale":"ABHA / HIP-HIU integration is not required for a standalone monitoring device but is recommended if the founder targets government hospitals or longitudinal record sharing."},"nabh":{"verdict":"conditional","rationale":"Becomes relevant if selling into NABH-accredited hospitals; not relevant for a pure D2C or clinic-level deployment."},"mci_telemed":{"verdict":"not_applicable","rationale":"No doctor-patient consultation feature described; the app is a monitoring companion, not a telemedicine platform."},"irdai":{"verdict":"not_applicable","rationale":"No insurance distribution, aggregation, or underwriting feature."},"nabl":{"verdict":"not_applicable","rationale":"Spirometer is a measurement device, not an IVD; NABL lab accreditation is not directly in scope."}},"top_gaps":[{"dim":"quality_system","gap_title":"No ISO 13485 QMS detected","fix_action":"Engage an ISO 13485 consultant for a gap assessment and start building a documented QMS — this is foundational for a Class B manufacturing license.","severity":"high"},{"dim":"quality_system","gap_title":"No IEC 62304 software lifecycle documentation detected","fix_action":"Stand up an IEC 62304-compliant software development lifecycle (SDLC) with documented architecture, risk classification, and verification protocols for the companion app.","severity":"high"},{"dim":"submission_maturity","gap_title":"No CDSCO engagement or test license activity detected","fix_action":"Open a SUGAM account, prepare a pre-submission file with predicate analysis, and scope an MD-5 application via your state licensing authority.","severity":"high"}],"verdict":"This is likely a Class B medical device under CDSCO MDR 2017, with the app inheriting the hardware's class as SiMD. Foundational QMS, software lifecycle documentation, and CDSCO engagement are all missing — submission readiness is effectively zero today. Since your product has a hardware component, state FDA approval may also apply depending on your manufacturing setup.","why_regulated":"A spirometer is a notified diagnostic measurement device for a serious respiratory condition (COPD), which places it squarely inside CDSCO MDR's scope. The companion app, as software-in-a-medical-device, generally inherits the hardware's risk class.","post_2025_samd_gap":false,"trl":{"level":3,"stage":"early_stage_poc","track":"has_predicate","completion_pct":22,"next_milestone":"Freeze design, complete bench testing against IS/ISO standards for spirometers, and prepare an MD-12 test license submission (TRL 4).","rationale":"A working prototype is implied by the product description, but no detected signals around design freeze, bench testing, or CDSCO submission. Anchored conservatively to TRL 3 on the predicate track, since spirometers have well-established Indian and international comparables."},"tier0_card_tagline":"Class B medical device · pathway clear, foundations missing","tier1_teaser":"Tier 1 (₹499) maps your exact MD-5 pathway, predicate options, and a 90-day QMS sprint plan tailored to spirometer manufacturers.","tier2_teaser":"Tier 2 (₹25K) delivers a full submission-ready dossier: ISO 13485 + IEC 62304 templates, predicate device analysis, MD-5 application draft, and clinical performance study protocol."}

rows.one_liner: AI medical scribe that converts doctor-patient conversations into structured notes.
rows.label_rationale: AI medical scribing is documentation support if no clinical recommendations are generated. Class A may apply if summaries influence low-risk clinical management.

rows.one_liner: AI app that tracks wound healing progress from smartphone photos.
rows.label_rationale: Wound healing image tracking supports clinical monitoring. Class C may apply if it diagnoses infection, necrosis, or recommends treatment changes.

rows.one_liner: App that predicts fertility window using cycle history and symptoms.
rows.label_rationale: Cycle and fertility tracking is usually wellness if not contraceptive or infertility treatment software. Class B may apply if used for contraception or fertility treatment decisions.

rows.one_liner: Hospital AI system that predicts sepsis risk from EHR and lab data.
rows.label_rationale: Sepsis prediction relates to a critical condition and can drive urgent intervention. Class C may apply only with strict clinician confirmation and prioritization-only claims.

rows.one_liner: App that reminds patients to take medicines and rewards adherence.
rows.label_rationale: Medication reminders and rewards are adherence support, not treatment decision software. Class A may apply if positioned as low-risk patient management software.
